result_convention.magnitude_metric: *_mag_abs_diff is an absolute difference in linear magnitude unless dB is stated explicitly

port_families.validation_status: Limited to the uniform-grid one-cell impedance model. Analytic extractor, real V/I replay, three-case replay/passivity/reciprocity, and narrow rfx/OpenEMS PEC-box magnitude checks exist. Broad matched/open/short/load external validation is absent.
known_limits: nonuniform lumped-port S-parameter extraction is unsupported
forward(port_s11_freqs=...) is uniform and single-device only
TFSF, waveguide ports, and mixed port families are unsupported in the same S-parameter calculation
automatic runtime checks cover non-finite and excessive individual |S| values; they do not apply the full column-power guard
validation_gaps: missing broad mesh, frequency, and geometry validation range
numeric_metrics: analytic open/short/matched/resistive/reactive/RLC extractor max_abs_diff 7.91e-8 <= 2.20e-6
real two-port V/I replay over 9 frequencies and 2 ports: max_abs_diff 1.13e-7 <= 9.84e-7
three-case replay check: max replay diff 1.58e-7, max column power 0.971, max reciprocity diff 3.02e-7
three-case rfx/OpenEMS PEC-box position comparison: max case max_mag_abs_diff 0.11835 and max case mean_mag_abs_diff 0.06466
port_families.validated_scope: Uniform Yee grid and one-cell lumped impedance model. The listed external cases are narrow PEC-box comparisons, not a general calibrated-port validation.
caveats: extractor checks validate V/I-to-S algebra, not the full FDTD port calibration
V/I replay validates reproducibility, not broad absolute RF accuracy
one-cell feed results must not be generalized to a transmission-line mode

port_families.validation_status: Limited one-cell transverse-feed result. Uniform-grid V/I replay, replay/passivity/reciprocity, patch/OpenEMS magnitude, and a three-case mesh/length OpenEMS comparison exist. Absolute modal calibration remains limited. Nonuniform use is experimental.
known_limits: wire ports are one-cell transverse feed models, not full microstrip or modal ports
forward(port_s11_freqs=...) is uniform and single-device only
nonuniform regression and AD coverage are not external RF validation
automatic runtime checks cover non-finite and excessive individual |S| values; they do not apply the full column-power guard
validation_gaps: missing a resolved or explicitly bounded absolute calibration convention
missing broader external validation outside the cited probe-feed cases
numeric_metrics: patch/OpenEMS S11 magnitude over 1.5--3.4 GHz: max_mag_abs_diff 0.05318 and mean_mag_abs_diff 0.02750; phase not gated
three-case replay check: max replay diff 8.20e-8, max column power 0.979, max reciprocity diff 1.24e-6
real two-port V/I replay over 7 frequencies and 2 ports: max_abs_diff 7.82e-8 <= 9.80e-7
three OpenEMS mesh/length cases over dx 1--2 mm, wire length 4--8 mm, and 0.8--1.8 GHz: max_mag_abs_diff_across_cases 0.05212
port_families.validated_scope: Uniform-grid one-cell probe/wire feed in the cited resonance and two-port cases. Nonuniform use is experimental.
caveats: patch resonance evidence is stronger than absolute S11 calibration evidence
V/I replay validates reproducibility, not general calibrated-port accuracy
the off-diagonal replay follows the current per-cell impedance convention and is not modal-port calibration

result_type: MSLSMatrixResult.S / freqs / Z0 / beta / port_names / reliable
port_families.validation_status: E5-narrow for the uniform laplace/quasi-TEM thru and notch configurations. The committed OpenEMS notch comparison is characterized rather than tight. Nonuniform laplace/uniform mode has internal regression coverage only. Eigenmode is unsupported.
known_limits: nonuniform mode=laplace or mode=uniform has no external comparison
Result.s_params is not used; call compute_msl_s_matrix(...)
outside JAX tracing, reliable[p,k]=false invalidates S[:,p,k] for physical interpretation; true is not an accuracy guarantee
mode=eigenmode, SBP-SAT subgridding, ADI, TFSF, and mixed port families are unsupported
strong-reflector |S11| has a characterized staircase-Z0 floor of about 0.16--0.22
the automatic guard includes full driven-column power; passing it is not an accuracy guarantee
validation_gaps: missing external validation for nonuniform laplace/uniform mode
missing broader mesh, frequency, and substrate-cell validation outside the cited configurations
numeric_metrics: uniform thru-line: |S21| in (0.90, 1.05) and Re(Z0) in (40, 65) ohm for the cited dx=80 um setup
analytic notch case: frequency error 1.63%, notch depth -34.3 dB, median Re(Z0) 48.6 ohm
committed OpenEMS notch comparison at dx=50 um: notch frequency difference 5.8%, S21 linear-magnitude mean difference 0.105 and maximum difference 0.2172 over 2.5--6 GHz
raw three-probe replay: max_abs_diff 0 over 30 frequencies
port_families.validated_scope: Uniform Yee laplace/quasi-TEM MSL calculator for the cited thru and notch configurations. Nonuniform mode is experimental and eigenmode is unsupported.
caveats: the external notch comparison is characterized, not a tight cross-solver match
low-signal mask semantics are documented in docs/public/guide/probes-sparams.mdx#low-signal-msl-bins
do not generalize matched/thru/notch evidence to strong reflectors or arbitrary launches

port_families.validation_status: Broad magnitude evidence covers documented uniform single-mode rectangular guides: analytic fixtures across five WR bands and Palace WR-90 empty, PEC-short, and slab comparisons. Phase and junction evidence are narrower. Nonuniform single-mode normalize=True and normalize=flux run. A passed Palace magnitude comparison covers normalize=flux, graded-dy ratio 2, WR-90 empty/PEC-short/dielectric-slab cases, and 8.2--12.4 GHz; other nonuniform configurations remain experimental.
known_limits: compute_waveguide_s_matrix(...) requires at least two waveguide ports
multimode normalize=True is unsupported
nonuniform external magnitude evidence is limited to normalize=flux, graded-dy ratio 2, WR-90 empty/PEC-short/dielectric-slab cases, and 8.2--12.4 GHz
nonuniform eps_override and sigma_override AD are implemented only for normalize=flux; only eps_override has a nonuniform AD-vs-FD regression test, and that test is not RF validation
lumped ports, TFSF, and manual periodic-axis overrides are incompatible
branches, T-junctions, and septa require per-port matched straight-guide references and far-port placement; compact arbitrary junctions are not covered
the automatic guard includes full driven-column power; passing it is not an accuracy guarantee
validation_gaps: broader external comparisons for other nonuniform profiles, bands, modes, phase, and junctions
missing broad phase validation across the magnitude-tested uniform configurations
numeric_metrics: uniform empty guide max |S11| < 0.02 and max column power < 1.02
uniform PEC short min |S11| >= 0.99 and max |S11| < 1.03

cv11 gates: band-mean linear-magnitude difference < 0.10 for S11 and < 0.07 for S21; masked band-mean phase difference < 60 degrees where reference magnitude >= 0.30; maximum complex-S difference < 0.30
uniform Palace WR-90 empty/PEC-short/slab comparison over 8.2--12.4 GHz: max_mag_abs_diff 0.0707 and mean_mag_abs_diff 0.00943
uniform analytic WR-band slabs with normalize=flux: per-case |S11| differences 0.005--0.041
nonuniform analytic characterization over grading ratios 1--3, eps_r 2 and 4, and 8.2--12.4 GHz: max_mag_abs_diff 0.01561
stored nonuniform Palace characterization for WR-90 empty/PEC-short/slab: max_mag_abs_diff 0.07009 and mean_mag_abs_diff 0.01042; nonuniform RF status remains experimental
port_families.validated_scope: Uniform single-mode rectangular-guide magnitude for the documented WR-band setups. Nonuniform external magnitude evidence is limited to normalize=flux, graded-dy ratio 2, WR-90 empty/PEC-short/dielectric-slab cases, and 8.2--12.4 GHz. Other nonuniform profiles, bands, phase, multimode, and arbitrary junctions require separate evidence. Nonuniform eps_override and sigma_override AD are available only with normalize=flux.
caveats: broad uniform magnitude evidence does not imply equally broad phase accuracy
the stored nonuniform Palace result validates only its stated magnitude configurations; AD regression is not RF validation
junction results require the documented matched-reference geometry and far-port clearance
choose slab length and frequencies away from analytic reflection nulls and make slab length an integer number of cells
port_families.ad_evidence: Uniform checks: tests/test_sparam_ad_end_to_end.py::test_waveguide_s_matrix_ad_end_to_end, tests/test_waveguide_sparam_ad.py::test_wg_smatrix_assembly_ad_traceable, and tests/test_waveguide_flux_ad.py::test_flux_smatrix_grad_finite_and_fd_consistent. Nonuniform eps_override and sigma_override are normalize=flux only; tests/test_waveguide_nu_flux_ad.py finite-difference-checks eps_override, while sigma_override has no corresponding nonuniform AD-vs-FD test.

port_families.validation_status: The one-port TEM-line reflection API has broad analytic validation over termination, impedance, frequency, and mesh axes plus a broad MEEP short/open comparison. It is differentiable through eps_scale. The older single-plane V/I S-matrix API is deprecated and experimental.
known_limits: compute_coaxial_line_reflection(...) requires exactly one registered coaxial port with face='top' and no mixed port family
compute_coaxial_line_reflection(...) is not a general multi-port coaxial network solver
compute_coaxial_line_reflection(...) requires mode='3d', solver='yee', precision='float32', stencil_order=2, a uniform grid, and boundary='cpml' with cpml_layers > 0
compute_coaxial_line_reflection(...) requires positive CPML on both z faces, cpml_axes='z', CPML tokens on all six BoundarySpec faces, and no periodic boundary axes

compute_coaxial_line_reflection(...) constructs its line, TEM source, DFT planes, and termination; separately registered geometry, thin conductors, lumped RLC elements, probes, field monitors, NTFF boxes, and add_coaxial_* termination helpers are unsupported

the registered coaxial port contributes x/y position, face, radii, and waveform; its z position, pin_length, and impedance do not set the internally derived line layout or loads; use feed_impedance= and use dut_impedance= only with termination='matched'
probe_count must be an integer of at least three and all requested probe planes must fit between the DUT and source; the method raises instead of silently using fewer planes
use about four or more annulus cells; the committed method gate requires at least 3.5
compute_coaxial_s_matrix(...) can report non-physical |S11| > 1 for a lossless short
nonuniform, Floquet, TFSF, SBP-SAT, arbitrary launches, and mixed-port combinations are unsupported
Simulation.run() and Simulation.forward() reject high-level coaxial S-parameter requests
compute_coaxial_line_reflection(...) has no shared automatic passivity guard; status and residual diagnostics do not replace RF validation
validation_gaps: a broader claim for arbitrary launches or general multi-port coaxial networks requires a separate API and external validation
separate external validation for any nonuniform or mixed-port use
numeric_metrics: analytic 4--12 GHz short/open/matched/resistive 25/100 ohm checks at Z0 48.6 and 63 ohm: method max |Gamma| deviation 0.0372 <= 0.05 and max recurrence residual 0.00588 <= 0.03
matched single-cell resistor fixture is reported separately with max |Gamma| deviation 0.0929
MEEP short/open comparison over 4--12 GHz: max_mag_abs_diff 0.0628 and mean_mag_abs_diff 0.0235
end-to-end eps_scale AD versus finite difference discrepancy 2.6%
port_families.validated_scope: The self-contained TEM coaxial-line model using the documented Simulation, port, and method arguments, in float32 precision on a three-dimensional, second-order uniform Yee grid with positive CPML on both z faces, cpml_axes='z', CPML tokens on all six BoundarySpec faces, no periodic boundary axes, exactly one face='top' coaxial port, at least three requested probe planes that all fit before the source, and the termination, frequency, characteristic-impedance, and mesh ranges in the committed fixtures. Separately registered geometry, sources, monitors, termination helpers, arbitrary launches, face='bottom', mixed port families, and multi-port networks are outside this result.
caveats: do not use deprecated compute_coaxial_s_matrix(...) as evidence for the documented line-reflection result
the matched fixture is characterized but excluded from the method-magnitude gate because of the single-cell annular resistor
MEEP covers short/open; resistive cases are covered by the analytic transmission-line fixture
port_families.ad_evidence: eps_scale -> FDTD -> DFT plane accumulators -> modal voltage -> matrix-pencil reflection -> Gamma; tests/test_coax_end_to_end_ad.py::test_coax_reflection_grad_finite_and_fd_consistent

port_families.validation_status: Experimental Floquet/Bloch excitation and broadside diagnostic helpers only. Synthetic modal, real-FDTD replay, empty-space analytic-null, and homogeneous-slab analytic checks exist. There is no documented high-level calibrated Floquet S-parameter API or independent RCWA/full-wave validation.
known_limits: no documented Simulation Floquet S-parameter API
nonuniform meshes are unsupported
TFSF combination is unsupported
off-broadside calibrated results are not documented
validation_gaps: missing high-level calibrated port API and normalization contract
missing RCWA or independent periodic full-wave validation
missing mesh, frequency, scan-angle, and polarization validation range
numeric_metrics: empty-space analytic-null diagnostic: max_abs_diff 0.06067 <= 0.07 and mean_abs_diff 0.05306 <= 0.06
homogeneous-slab analytic oracle: 8 cases x 3 frequencies and max power-balance error 4.44e-16
rfx-FDTD homogeneous-slab magnitude diagnostic: max_mag_abs_diff 0.06212 <= 0.07 and mean_mag_abs_diff 0.03209 <= 0.04 over 3 frequencies
synthetic specular-TE check: S11 diff 2.89e-7, S21 diff 6.37e-7, power-balance error 1.02e-6
real-FDTD Ex/Hy DFT-plane replay: max S diff 2.23e-7
port_families.validated_scope: Experimental broadside excitation and diagnostic decomposition only. No calibrated public Floquet-port S-parameter result is documented.
caveats: analytic empty-space and slab checks are not RCWA or independent full-wave validation
modal replay validates bookkeeping, not a calibrated reference-plane result
Simulation.run() and Simulation.forward() reject high-level Floquet S-parameter requests
port_families.ad_evidence: finite-gradient checks through compute_floquet_s_params and extract_floquet_modes in tests/test_floquet_s_params_contract.py; this is an AD execution check, not an RF-accuracy statement

numeric_metrics: not an S-parameter calculation
caveats: do not use as a substitute for a calibrated port
